data.title: As an engineering team lead, I want kudos-snapshot.sh to capture pre-session test state at worktree creation
data.description:
As an engineering team lead, I need the pre-session test state captured at worktree creation before any test mutations occur so that T1/T2/T4 triggers have a reliable baseline for comparison.

DONE DEFINITIONS:
- kudos-snapshot.sh exists at plugins/dso/hooks/lib/kudos-snapshot.sh; executable bit set
- Reads test-gate-status from $ARTIFACTS_DIR/test-gate-status (from get_artifacts_dir())
- Snapshot schema per S2 spike decision (Option A or B); stores merge_base from git merge-base HEAD main at snapshot time
- When test-gate-status absent: writes empty snapshot with captured_at (ISO-8601 UTC) and merge_base
- Uses flock -s (shared lock) on test-gate-status for atomic read; documents best-effort consistency (write-side locking not enforced in record-test-status.sh)
- Output written to .kudos/test-gate-snapshot.json (gitignored per Epic 1 Story A)
- Registration: new dso-config.conf key kudos.snapshot_enabled (default true); worktree-create.sh invokes kudos-snapshot.sh after post_create_cmd, separate invocation (does NOT modify existing post_create_cmd value)
- Unit tests cover: absent test-gate-status, valid test-gate-status, kudos.snapshot_enabled=false (skip), worktree-create.sh integration order

CONSIDERATIONS:
- [Blocker] Depends on S2 spike — snapshot schema determined by spike result
- [Architecture] .kudos/ path relative to REPO_ROOT (not per-agent worktree); .kudos/ gitignored so worktree git status --porcelain remains clean
- [Safeguard] worktree-create.sh is a safeguard file (rule 18); task description must explicitly name worktree-create.sh as target
